title: Volunteer Legal Researcher
summary: Research questions the mission keeps running into: comparative court procedure, ODR frameworks, and what other justice systems have tried.
body:
## The role

Every design decision in a people-centric court hides a research question: how do other jurisdictions serve summons digitally, what does the case law say about asynchronous hearings, what happened when another country tried this. Volunteers who can answer such questions well shape what gets built.

[draft description, refine before publishing]

## What you'll do

- Take on scoped research questions and return clear, sourced answers
- Contribute to public knowledge products: explainers, briefs, learning circles
- Join a community of 100+ contributors across law, technology and government

## Who this suits

- Law students, researchers or practitioners with a few hours a week
- Rigorous sourcing and readable writing

## How it works

Volunteer role, remote and flexible. Contributions are credited publicly.
